evals.expected_output: Should detect repo, pick target, create PR
expectations: Detects current repo and branch
Recommends target branch (feature→dev, dev→beta, etc.)
Runs local quality checks if applicable
Creates PR via gh CLI with proper format
Detects branch protection on target
Validates required status checks exist
Warns about bootstrap deadlock if checks missing
Suggests alternative target if needed
Runs composer check:strict for PHP apps
Runs npm lint for frontend
Reports failures before creating PR
Suggests fixes
Does NOT create PR with failing checks

evals.description: PR title uses Conventional Commits prefix
evals.setup: Repo has merged PRs with feat:/fix:/docs: prefixes
evals.expected_output: Should detect convention and apply prefix to title
expectations: Runs gh pr list --state merged to detect title convention
Drafts title with Conventional Commits prefix (feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:)
Does NOT draft a title with no prefix
Test plan uses plain bullets, never - [ ] checkboxes

evals.description: Warn when target branch is far ahead
evals.prompt: Create PR from a branch that is 30+ commits behind development
evals.setup: Source branch diverged significantly from target; git log shows 30+ commits ahead on target
evals.expected_output: Should warn and offer merge/rebase before drafting
expectations: Computes how many commits target is ahead of source
Warns prominently when target is >20 commits ahead
Offers merge/rebase/continue-anyway options
Does NOT silently proceed to PR draft with a bloated diff

should_trigger: I need to create a PR
can you open a PR for this branch
submit my changes as a pull request
create a PR from this feature branch
create pull request for the current changes
I'm done with the feature, create a PR
should_not_trigger: assign a reviewer to the PR
check CI status on the PR
